metadata.title: Landing Page Redesign with Hero Section

story.iWant: a modern, visually appealing landing page with clear value proposition and format badges
story.soThat: I immediately understand Recipe's purpose, see supported formats at a glance, and feel confident using the tool

criterion.title: Hero Section with Clear Value Proposition
criterion.description: Hero section at top with headline, subheadline, call-to-action button
criterion.verification: - Headline: "Convert Photo Presets. Instantly. Privately." - Subheadline explains Recipe's purpose (1-2 sentences max) - Clean typography using system fonts - Visually distinct from main content - "Get Started" button scrolls to upload section

criterion.title: Visual Format Badges
criterion.description: Format badges with defined brand colors prominently displayed
criterion.verification: - 5 badges: NP3 (#FFC107), XMP (#0073E6), lrtemplate (#D81B60), Capture One (#9C27B0), DCP (#4CAF50) - Shows format abbreviation and full name on hover - Consistent styling (rounded corners, bold text, white text on colored background) - Accessible: Color not sole indicator

criterion.title: Single-Page Layout
criterion.description: All core functionality on single page with smooth scroll behavior
criterion.verification: - Sections: Hero, Format badges, Upload, Conversion - Smooth scroll between sections (CSS scroll-behavior: smooth) - No page reloads during conversion (SPA behavior)

criterion.title: Responsive Design
criterion.verification: - Mobile (320px-767px): Single column, headline 28px, vertical badges - Tablet (768px-1023px): 2-column grid, headline 36px, wrapped badges - Desktop (1024px+): Centered 1200px, headline 48px, badges in row - Touch-friendly 44x44px minimum tap targets

criterion.title: Performance - Fast Load Time
criterion.description: Page loads in less than 2 seconds on 3G connection
criterion.verification: - WebPageTest validation on 3G (1.6 Mbps, 300ms RTT) - Zero external dependencies (no CDN fonts, no analytics) - CSS minified and inline (or single stylesheet less than 10KB) - JavaScript deferred or async - WASM loaded on-demand - Progressive enhancement: basic HTML/CSS works without JavaScript

criterion.title: Clean Typography
criterion.description: System font stack with defined font sizes and line heights
criterion.verification: - System fonts: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto - Font sizes via CSS variables (base: 16px, large: 20px, hero: 48px responsive) - Line heights: body 1.6, headings 1.2 - Font weights: normal 400, bold 600

criterion.title: No External Dependencies
criterion.description: Zero third-party scripts or stylesheets, all self-hosted
criterion.verification: - No Google Fonts, Bootstrap, Tailwind, or CSS frameworks - No jQuery or utility libraries - No analytics (Google Analytics, Cloudflare Web Analytics) - All assets in web/ directory - Privacy-focused: Zero tracking, zero external requests

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: CSS Variables for format badge colors (NP3=#FFC107, XMP=#0073E6, lrtemplate=#D81B60, Capture One=#9C27B0, DCP=#4CAF50), typography variables, spacing system, BEM naming convention for CSS classes

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: Hero section design pattern, format badge desktop layout (5 badges in row), component structure for web/ directory with CSS modules (main.css, components.css, layout.css)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: 3G load time target less than 2 seconds, optimization strategies (system fonts, inline critical CSS, lazy load WASM), performance monitoring with Lighthouse CI (target ≥95), WebPageTest validation

doc.title: Product Requirements Document - Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Design philosophy: Craft Over Flash, Confidence Through Clarity, Privacy-First Transparency, Progressive Disclosure. Visual personality: Modern but Timeless, Format Badge System with defined color meanings

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Project structure for web/ directory, CSS architecture (main.css for variables, components.css for badges/buttons, layout.css for responsive grid), responsive breakpoints (mobile less than 768px, tablet 768-1024px, desktop greater than 1024px)

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Color palette CSS variables, responsive breakpoints with mobile-first approach, BEM naming convention (.block__element--modifier), format badge colors with accessibility notes (yellow/green need dark text)

file.reason: Current landing page HTML structure to be modified - contains hero section, landing content sections, footer structure, and existing privacy/legal sections to preserve
Existing app initialization pattern - will need to integrate with new CSS modules, maintains WASM loading and event-driven architecture
Existing stylesheet to refactor into modular CSS files (main.css, components.css, layout.css) - extract variables, component styles, and responsive patterns
Privacy-first messaging patterns already implemented - ensure hero section aligns with existing privacy communication style
Touch detection and orientation handling already implemented - ensure CSS responsive design works with existing JavaScript enhancements

package.name: vanilla-javascript
package.usage: No framework - modern JavaScript with modules

constraints.constraint: Page load time must be less than 2 seconds on 3G connection (1.6 Mbps, 300ms RTT) - validated with WebPageTest
Zero external dependencies - no CDN fonts, no analytics trackers, no external CSS/JS libraries
Vanilla JavaScript only - no React, Vue, Svelte, or other frameworks
Basic HTML/CSS must work without JavaScript - core message (hero, format badges) visible without JS
Support Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+ - no IE11 support needed
Touch targets must be minimum 44x44px on mobile per Apple/Google guidelines
Mobile-first CSS approach - base styles for mobile, media queries for larger screens
BEM naming convention for CSS classes (.block__element--modifier)
CSS modules organized by purpose (main.css global, components.css reusable, layout.css responsive grid)

interface.name: Hero Section Component
interface.signature:
Convert Photo Presets. Instantly. Privately.

Transform your photo editing presets...

Get Started

interface.name: Format Badge Component
interface.signature: NP3 CSS: .badge { display: inline-block; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border-radius: 6px; } CSS: .badge--np3 { background-color: var(--color-np3); }

tests.standards: Epic 10 focuses on frontend UI/UX with manual testing emphasis. Testing standards: Lighthouse audit for performance/accessibility (target ≥95), WebPageTest for 3G load time (target less than 2 seconds), browser compatibility testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge), responsive design testing at defined breakpoints (320px, 768px, 1024px), accessibility testing with keyboard navigation and screen readers (VoiceOver, NVDA), visual regression testing for format badges and hero section
tests.locations: Manual testing documented in story completion notes. Performance benchmarks: WebPageTest (3G), Lighthouse CI. Browser compatibility: BrowserStack or manual testing. Responsive testing: Chrome DevTools device emulation + real devices (iPhone, Android, iPad)

test.idea: Manual: Verify hero section displays correctly with headline, subheadline, and CTA button. Test "Get Started" button smooth scroll to #upload section
Lighthouse: Verify hero section passes accessibility audit (color contrast, semantic HTML)
Manual: Verify all 5 format badges display with correct colors (visual inspection against hex values). Test hover interaction shows full format name
Accessibility: Verify badges pass WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 minimum) - note yellow/green badges may need dark text adjustment
Manual: Test smooth scroll from hero CTA to upload section. Verify no page reloads during workflow (check Network tab for zero full-page requests)
Functional: Test that all sections (hero, formats, upload, conversion) are accessible on single page without navigation
Manual: Test responsive breakpoints in Chrome DevTools (320px, 768px, 1024px). Verify hero title font-size changes (28px→36px→48px) and badge layout adapts
Device: Test on real devices (iPhone SE, iPad, desktop) to verify touch targets ≥44x44px and no horizontal scrolling
WebPageTest: Test load time on 3G connection (1.6 Mbps, 300ms RTT) - target less than 2 seconds. Verify First Contentful Paint less than 1.5s, Largest Contentful Paint less than 2.0s
Network: Verify zero external requests (check Network tab - all assets self-hosted in web/ directory)
Lighthouse: Run audit targeting Performance ≥95, verify no render-blocking resources
Manual: Verify typography renders correctly with system fonts on Windows (Segoe UI), macOS (San Francisco), Linux (Roboto)
Visual: Verify font sizes match CSS variables (base: 16px, large: 20px, hero: 48px on desktop). Check line-height readability
Network: Inspect Network tab in browser DevTools - verify zero requests to external domains (no CDN fonts, no analytics scripts)
Code Review: Audit index.html for external <link> or <script> tags - confirm all assets are relative paths in web/ directory
Privacy: Test offline mode (disable network) - verify landing page loads and displays correctly (after initial load)
